# Farha vs. WishApp (2026 Comparison)

Farha compared with WishApp, two wishlist apps side by side

Farha is a brand-new wishlist app from a solo founder, built around occasions and a few features WishApp doesn't have. Here is how the two stack up on price, the devices you can use, and the gifting tools that decide which one sticks.

Last updated: June 2026

Looking for a Farha alternative? Farha is a free, very new app with two real standouts: 24/7 price drop alerts and group gifting that WishApp doesn't match. But it's iOS-only today, with no Android and no browser extension. WishApp runs everywhere and is proven. Among Farha alternatives, WishApp is the one we'd pick.

## Feature comparison [...] ### Gifting and group features

Farha and WishApp split this one. Farha has two things WishApp doesn't: group gifting, where several people pool money toward one big item, and thank-you notes you send after the gift arrives. Both are thoughtful. WishApp goes wider instead, with collaborative lists where a couple can co-manage one wishlist, a follow system, a discover feed, and full reserved-and-received tracking with a note like 'from Sarah'. Both name Secret Santa as an occasion, but neither draws names automatically yet.

### Ease of use and trust [...] ### Sharing, privacy and reservations

Both apps get the core right. Share a list with one link, and friends open it in a browser with no account needed. Both hide reservations so the owner never sees who claimed what, which is the whole point of a gift list. Farha adds a nice touch here: it supports Arabic with full right-to-left layout, plus French, which very few wishlist apps bother with. WishApp answers with QR codes, all currencies, and a wider language reach on web, but no Arabic. For a MENA or francophone family, Farha is genuinely the friendlier option.

### Gifting and group features

results.title: 10 Best Universal Wishlist Apps in 2026, Compared and Ranked | Farha
results.content:
Not anymore. Amazon discontinued universal wishlist support in March 2023 when it shut down Amazon Assistant. Amazon Wishlist now only works with products sold on Amazon.com.

For the most complete free experience, Farha offers universal store support, anonymous reservations, group gifting, and price tracking with no premium tier. Giftster and Things To Get Me are also fully free.

Privacy practices vary by app. Most earn revenue through affiliate links. Read each app's privacy policy before signing up. Giftwhale is explicitly privacy-first, and Farha does not sell user data to third parties. [...] ### Can I use Amazon Wishlist for items from other stores?

Not anymore. Amazon used to support adding items from external websites through the Amazon Assistant browser extension. In March 2023, Amazon discontinued this feature. Amazon Wishlist now only works with products sold on Amazon.com. For a multi-store alternative, see our guide to Amazon wishlist alternatives.

### What is the best free wishlist app?

It depends on your needs. For the most complete free experience with no premium tier, Farha, Giftster, and Things To Get Me offer all features at no cost. Farha stands out with universal store support, group gifting, price tracking, and anonymous reservations, all included in the free tier. See our best free wishlist apps comparison for a deeper look. [...] ## 3. Giftster -- Best for families

Giftster has been around since 2008 and has over 2 million registered users, mostly US-based families. The app is built around family circles where members maintain wishlists for recurring events like birthdays and holidays.

Key features:

 Family circle structure with member profiles and event calendars.
 Claiming system so family members can mark gifts as purchased.
 Recurring event reminders for birthdays and anniversaries.
 Works across generations with a straightforward interface.

Best for: Large US families who want a dedicated family gifting platform with event tracking. See our Farha vs Giftster comparison for details.

results.title: Farha vs Giftster: Which Wishlist App Is Better for Families?
results.content:
Farha: Completely free. All features, including universal adding, price tracking, group gifting, anonymous reservations, and multilingual support, are available at no cost.

Giftster: Also completely free. Family circles, event calendars, claiming, and list management are all included without a subscription or paywall.

Neither app uses a freemium model that locks essential features behind a paywall. You won't hit a wall where the app suddenly asks you to upgrade. Both companies have chosen to make their full feature sets available for free.

This is unusual in the app market, so take advantage of it. You can try both without spending anything.

## Who should use Giftster? [...] Choose Giftster if your family is US-based, already using the platform, and values a dedicated family circle with event calendars and member profiles. Giftster does this well and has for years. Its 2 million-plus user base is proof that the model works.

Choose Farha if your family shops across many stores, spans multiple countries or languages, or wants modern features like price tracking and group gifting. Farha's universal approach means you're not limited to one retailer or one country.

Both apps are free. You lose nothing by trying each one and seeing which fits your family's actual habits. The best wishlist app is the one your family will actually use consistently.

## Frequently asked questions [...] Both apps work in a web browser. With Farha, gift viewers don't need to download an app or create an account. Giftster requires account creation for all family members but has a straightforward interface.

results.title: 7 Best Wishlist Apps to Share with Friends in 2026 (No ... - Farha
results.content:
## Which app is right for your friend group?

For birthday coordination with minimal setup: Farha. Your friends get a link, open it in their browser, reserve what they're buying, and optionally chip in on something bigger. No sign-up required for them, and you never see who claimed what until the gift arrives.

For annual Secret Santa or gift exchanges: Elfster. The name draw and exchange management is unmatched. Use Farha alongside it for year-round birthday wishlists.

For recurring close friend groups that gift together regularly: Giftster. The group structure and established user base suit tight circles that want long-term list management. [...] The gifting coordination features are minimal. There are no anonymous reservations, no group gifting, and no duplicate prevention. Moonsift is excellent for communicating aesthetic preferences — "here's the kind of thing I like" — but it doesn't handle the coordination side of friend-group gifting. Pair it with a coordination tool if you need both.

### Pros

 Beautiful image-driven wishlist layout
 Strong privacy controls (private or public per list)
 Works across any store
 Good browser extension for quick saving

### Cons

 No anonymous reservations — surprises are not protected
 No group gifting
 Requires accounts for active collaboration
 Not a gifting coordination tool

### Pricing

Free basic tier. Premium for additional features. [...] Yes. Farha's group gifting feature lets multiple friends each contribute a portion toward one item on your list. It's completely free — no premium tier required. Giftwhale also offers a split-payment group gifting feature on its free plan.
